mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand across consumption and investment channels, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns during early-cycle phases, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 60% of high-inflation trigger months or the signal predictive power vanishes when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic resilience and supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields a negative average forward return or a Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent contractions in real GDP and retail sales indicate a synchronized demand shock that depresses corporate profit forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined demand contraction fails to predict negative returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 50% of triggered cases in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side stress and inventory drawdowns, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold breach is followed by positive returns in more than 55% of instances or the signal shows no predictive edge over a random baseline.
